content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach largely avoids the major AoA anti-patterns: no evangelizing of methodology, no selling AoA terminology, no defensive 'but this work makes you efficient' pivot, no pragmatic fixing, and a clean refusal of the dichotomy at turn 4 ('That's a real cost. I'm not arguing with it'). However, turn 8 ('What's the feeling you've been avoiding about this situation?') and especially turn 12 edge into a mild form of fixing/loving-to-transform — turn 12 makes a somewhat declarative case for why the emotional and practical aren't separate, which functions a bit like selling the relevance of the work after the client just pushed back. It's not full evangelizing (no methodology language, no 'once you feel into this...'), but it does assert the frame rather than meet the resistance with a softer 'okay.' Turn 8 also has a faint wonder-as-strategy quality — a probing question that bypasses the resistance the client just expressed in turn 7 rather than meeting it. None of this rises to a clear major trip, but it's more than zero, so 2 fits better than 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reasonable opening question; no anti-pattern."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clean refusal of the dichotomy without arguing it ('I'm not arguing with it'). Good positive avoidance of defending-the-methodology."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Avoids evangelizing or selling; gently names the defense without making it a thing. 'I'm not trying to catch you' avoids the catch-them anti-pattern."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Pivot to 'what feeling are you avoiding' feels slightly like wonder-as-strategy — a probing investigation rather than a meeting move, given the client's strong stated resistance."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Following the content; no anti-pattern."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Edges toward selling/defending the methodology by asserting the emotional-practical link ('Those two things aren't as separate as you're making them...investors read state'). This is a mild form of arguing for why the work matters, rather than meeting resistance with okay."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["i-dont-buy-your-story", "the-rush-signature", "feel-your-way-to-freedom"],
  "notes": "Closest brush is turn 12, which mildly defends the relevance of feeling work to the practical problem. Not a clear evangelizing trip, but enough to keep this from a clean 3."
}
